title: After Action Review — AI Kicking Snap Kicks on Early Downs While Trailing
scope: Fix the coaching AI kicking snap kicks on downs 1-3 (especially while trailing), enforce the principle that kicks belong on 6th down, and close all code paths that allow early-down kicks.

rationale.item: Incident: Kansas State 36.0 vs Cincinnati 35.5 (Week 10 CONF). Cincinnati kicked five snap kicks including two on 3rd down in Q4 while trailing. Final drive: Cincinnati drove 11 plays for 62 yards reaching the 92 yard line — 8 yards from the end zone. On 3rd & 12, the AI kicked a snap kick for 5 points instead of continuing to drive for a 9-point touchdown. Cincinnati had 4th, 5th, and 6th down still available. Lost by 0.5 points.

delegation_events.decision: Kicks belong on 6th down — Downs 1-4 advance the ball, Down 5 is last advancement attempt, Down 6 is the kicking down
delegation_events.quote: Kicking on any down before 6th is like punting on 3rd down in the NFL — you're surrendering downs for no reason.

actions.description: Bug 1 fix: re-apply kick gate after Boot Raid weights_attack override to prevent snap_kick=0.35 overwriting down gate
Bug 2 fix: removed entire downs 2-3 probabilistic pull-up snap kick path (_check_snap_kick_shot_play() returns None for downs 1-3)
Bug 3 fix: down 4 returns None except desperation clock + bonus possession; removed kick mode activation
Down 5 kick logic: only on bonus possessions or clock pressure
New final enforcement gate in select_play_family(): re-zeros kick weights on downs 1-5 after ALL modifiers
Early gate extended from downs 1-3 to downs 1-5 for snap_kick zeroing

risk_posture: Down 4 now returns None (keep driving) in virtually all cases. The only exception is desperation clock (< 45 seconds at end of half) AND a bonus possession.
open_hooks: Defense in depth for weight gates: new final safety gate at bottom of select_play_family() ensures no downstream code — current or future — can re-introduce kick weight on early downs
section_headers_original: After Action Review — AI Kicking Snap Kicks on Early Downs While Trailing
Bug 1 — Boot Raid Attack Weight Override Bypasses Down Gate
Bug 2 — Pull-Up-Three Has No Deficit Awareness
unmapped_content: Branch: claude/fix-ai-football-decisions-AlCfC. Commit: 39f7c18. 1 file changed, +87/-217 lines. Changes Summary table showing before/after for all 7 change areas. Lessons: (1) Style overrides need gate enforcement — wholesale weight replacement bypasses all prior gates; (2) Opportunistic kick systems need game awareness — AI had no concept of game flow; (3) Specialist multipliers compound dangerously — Boot Raid (1.5x) × specialist (1.6x) × high skill (1.1x) = 37% per-play probability; (4) Defense in depth for weight gates. Play-by-play evidence: Q3 9:32 CIN 91 2&12 SNAP KICK 19yd GOOD; Q3 2:19 CIN 80 1&20 SNAP KICK 30yd GOOD; Q4 9:06 CIN 77 3&8 SNAP KICK 33yd GOOD trailing ~6; Q4 1:44 CIN 92 3&12 SNAP KICK 18yd GOOD trailing ~1.
parser_notes: Date confirmed from document header: 2026-03-18. No explicit validation section — changes summary table shows before/after but no batch sim results. No non_actions.
